title: AI Result Accuracy — Competitor Diligence Questions
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir drafting a diligence question list for an AI-visibility competitor — the central ask: have they made the API-to-scraping shift so their audit "sees what LLM front-end bots see"?
  • Argues most audit tools fall short because API results diverge from what real users get; accuracy is the moat question.
  • Wants to understand technical implementation across customer tiers/phases, plus team makeup (venture-backed vs. bootstrapped, headcount).
  • Drills into commercial reality: success metrics that can be put in front of a client, marketing channels, biggest closing blockers, fulfillment systemization.
  • Tools/people in margin: Lovable (likely build tool reference) and "Jeffntc-bird" (likely Jeff at NTC Bird contact).

Notes

This is Rankett-mode Yahir doing competitive intelligence — not a tweet-reaction, not a manifesto, just a structured set of questions to drag into a call. It's the operational counterpart to the existential "are there people pursuing this with all their soul?" anxiety from the January Rankett note. There he was asking whether anyone was real; here he's asking how a specific player handles the hardest technical problem.

The framing puts AI visibility accuracy on a real architectural axis: API-based answers (what the LLM provider returns) vs. scraping-based answers (what front-end bots actually display to a user). Yahir's bet is that the second is the only thing that matters because that's what clients can see. This is a good question to ask — it separates serious operators from dashboard-builders.

Repeated "how are yall systemizing fulfillment" three times in slightly different ways is Yahir interrogating his own gap, not just the competitor's — fulfillment systemization keeps showing up across the agency-side notes.

Connections

  • rankett — central project the diligence feeds.
  • ai visibility engine — the category being audited.
  • 2026 01 13 rankettcom — sister note, existential vs. operational version of the same anxiety.
  • asking the right question — the API-vs-scraping cut is a cleaner framing than most.
  • openai api — referenced indirectly as the limited-visibility surface.
  • lovable — build-tool reference in margin.
